info.title: Sendry API
info.description: Sendry is a modern email sending API for transactional and marketing email. Authenticate with a Bearer token using your API key.

tags.name: Deliverability
tags.description: Reputation tracking, blocklist monitoring, and deliverability reports

tags.name: Dedicated IPs
tags.description: Dedicated IP address provisioning and warmup management

tags.name: Regions
tags.description: Multi-region SES sending configuration and data residency

BearerAuth.description: Use your Sendry API key as a Bearer token. Create API keys in the dashboard or via the API Keys endpoint.

changes.description: List of changes in this release

get.description: Returns the current operational status of all Sendry components and any active incidents.

last_checked_at.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent health check, or null if worker never ran

sla_target.description: SLA uptime target as a percentage (e.g. 99.95)

sla_met.description: Whether current 90-day uptime meets the SLA target

current_response_time_ms.description: Latest health check response time in ms

current_uptime.description: Current average uptime across all components

sla_met.description: Whether the system is currently meeting its SLA

get.description: Returns a paginated list of resolved incidents ordered by most recent first.
Returns hourly P50/P95/P99 latency rollups for a component over the specified time window.

schema.description: Number of hours of history to return

target_ms.description: Target median response time in milliseconds

current_p50_ms.description: Current P50 latency in ms, or null if no data

target_met.description: Whether current P50 is within target

hour.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for this hour bucket

target_met_pct.description: Percentage of requests within target in this hour

get.summary: Get BIMI configuration for a domain

schema.description: Unique key for idempotent sends. Same key within 24h returns the cached response.

from.description: Sender email address. Format: 'Name <email@example.com>' or 'email@example.com'. Domain must be verified.

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Single string or array of up to 50 addresses.

subject.description: Email subject line. Required unless template_id is provided (the template's subject will be used).

html.description: HTML body of the email. Either html or text (or both) must be provided unless using a template.

text.description: Plain text body of the email

tags.description: Custom tags for filtering and analytics

headers.description: Custom email headers. Only X-* header names are accepted.

scheduled_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime or natural language expression (e.g. 'in 2 hours', 'Friday at 9am EST') for future delivery. Maximum 30 days in advance.

template_id.description: ID of a saved template to use. Overrides html/text fields.

variables.description: Template variables for substitution when using template_id

content_id.description: Content-ID for inline embedding. Reference in HTML as cid:YOUR_ID

tracking.description: Enable open/click tracking. Defaults to true.

topic_id.description: Topic ID. If set, the email is only sent to recipients who are subscribed to this topic (or not explicitly unsubscribed, for opt-out topics).

post.description: Immediately enqueue a draft or scheduled campaign for sending.
Move a scheduled campaign back to draft so it can be edited or rescheduled.
Cancel a scheduled or paused campaign.
Pause a campaign that is currently sending.
Resume sending a paused campaign.

get.description: List all recipients for a campaign with their delivery status and timestamps. Supports cursor-based pagination and status filtering.

schema.description: Number of results per page (1-100, default 50)

anyOf.description: Number of results per page (1-100, default 50)

schema.description: Filter by recipient status (pending, queued, sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, complained, unsubscribed, failed, skipped)

post.description: Provision a new dedicated IP address for email sending. Available on Business and Enterprise plans.

get.description: List all dedicated IP addresses for your organization.
Retrieve details for a specific dedicated IP including domain assignments and warmup progress.

delete.description: Release a dedicated IP address. This will delete the associated SES pool and all domain assignments.

post.description: Assign a dedicated IP to a domain. The IP must be in warming or active status.

delete.description: Remove a dedicated IP assignment from a domain.

get.description: Returns all active SES regions available for sending. Use the region_code value when setting region preferences.
Returns the organization-level default SES sending region and data residency constraint.

patch.summary: Update org default region and data residency
patch.description: Set the organization-level default SES sending region and optional data residency constraint.
Override the SES sending region for a specific domain. Takes priority over the org default region.

get.description: Returns a breakdown of sent emails by SES region for the specified date range.
Returns the SMTP server connection details. Use any API key with sending_access scope as the SMTP password.

examples: Product feature announcements and release notes

post.description: Create a new automation in draft status. Use the steps endpoints to build out the workflow before activating.

patch.description: Edit only when the automation is in draft or paused status.

delete.description: Only draft or archived automations may be deleted.

post.description: Create and enqueue a new run for an active automation, bypassing the configured trigger.
Ingest an event for automation triggering. Optional `event_id` is used for idempotency: a duplicate event_id returns the original event with `deduped: true`.
